[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take / sellerPendingFeeDecrease] Can an unprivileged taker cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease = sellerPos.pendingFee.mulDivUp(sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) to round up and reduce the seller's pendingFee by more than proportional, causing the continuous fee that would have accrued on the consumed credit to be permanently lost (never added to continuousFeeCredit), resulting in underclaimed continuous fees? Proof idea: fuzz sellerCreditDecrease vs sellerPos.credit, assert sum of sellerPendingFeeDecrease across partial fills <= original pendingFee, assert continuousFeeCredit not reduced by sellerPendingFeeDecrease.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / withdraw / pendingFeeDecrease] Can an unprivileged lender cause pendingFeeDecrease = pendingFee.mulDivUp(units, credit) in withdraw() to round up and reduce pendingFee by more than proportional, causing the continuous fee that would have accrued on the withdrawn credit to be permanently lost (never added to continuousFeeCredit), resulting in underclaimed continuous fees? Proof idea: fuzz partial withdrawal amounts, assert sum of pendingFeeDecrease across withdrawals <= original pendingFee, assert continuousFeeCredit delta == expected accrual on remaining credit.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee with amount == continuousFeeCredit, causing _marketState.withdrawable -= amount to underflow if withdrawable < continuousFeeCredit (because withdrawable tracks repaid/liquidated units while continuousFeeCredit accumulates accrued fees independently), making the claim revert and continuous fees permanently unclaimable? Proof idea: accrue continuousFeeCredit via updatePosition without corresponding repay/liquidate, call claimContinuousFee with full continuousFeeCredit, assert no underflow on withdrawable.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee with amount == continuousFeeCredit, causing _marketState.totalUnits -= amount to underflow if totalUnits < continuousFeeCredit (because totalUnits decreases via withdrawals while continuousFeeCredit accumulates), making the claim revert and continuous fees permanently unclaimable? Proof idea: lend, accrue fees, withdraw all credit (totalUnits decreases), call claimContinuousFee, assert totalUnits >= continuousFeeCredit before claim.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / liquidate / continuousFeeCredit] Can an unprivileged liquidator cause continuousFeeCredit to be reduced disproportionately during bad debt realization via the mulDivDown scaling (_marketState.continuousFeeCredit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - _marketState.lossFactor, type(uint128).max - _lossFactor)), making previously accrued continuous fees unclaimable even though the corresponding pendingFee was already deducted from lenders' credit? Proof idea: accrue continuousFeeCredit, realize bad debt, assert continuousFeeCredit after liquidation >= sum of pendingFee reductions already applied to lenders.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take / _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged taker cause the continuous fee to be charged to the wrong side by having a seller with credit (hasCredit returns true) trigger _updatePosition for the seller before sellerCreditDecrease is applied, accruing continuous fees on the full pre-take credit, while the seller's credit is then reduced by sellerCreditDecrease, causing the seller to be charged continuous fees on credit they no longer hold after the take? Proof idea: seller has credit, take() reduces seller credit, assert _updatePosition called before credit reduction, assert accruedFee computed on pre-take credit, assert this is the intended behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken with units=0 (no-op take), result in 0 settlement fee being accumulated and consumed[maker][group] unchanged, but the take() still calls callbacks and ratifiers, potentially allowing a callback to manipulate state that affects future settlement fee accounting? Proof idea: call take() with units=0, assert claimableSettlementFee unchanged, assert consumed unchanged, assert no state changes that affect future fee accounting.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken, result in buyerAssets - sellerAssets being accumulated in claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] while the actual token transfer is buyerAssets - sellerAssets from payer to address(this), and verify that the contract's loanToken balance always satisfies balance >= claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] as an invariant after every take()? Proof idea: fuzz N takes with varying units and fees, assert contract.balanceOf(loanToken) >= claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] after each take().

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer call claimSettlementFee with amount > contract.balanceOf(loanToken) (if claimableSettlementFee was inflated by rounding in take() where buyerAssets - sellerAssets > actual fee paid), causing the transfer to drain tokens belonging to lenders or borrowers, resulting in insolvency? Proof idea: assert contract.balanceOf(loanToken) >= claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] as invariant, fuzz N takes, assert no rounding in buyerAssets - sellerAssets causes claimableSettlementFee to exceed actual tokens received.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken in a multicall where setMarketSettlementFee is called first (in the same transaction), result in the settlement fee being computed with the new fee in take() but the old fee in consumableUnits (called before the multicall), causing a discrepancy in the units returned and the actual settlement fee accumulated? Proof idea: call consumableUnits off-chain, then multicall [setMarketSettlementFee, take(consumableUnits_result)], assert claimableSettlementFee delta uses new fee and consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer at TTM = exactly 1 day (86400 seconds) cause consumableUnits to use the 1d-7d interpolation (timeToMaturity < 7 days, >= 1 day) while a 1-second warp causes take() to use the 0d-1d interpolation (timeToMaturity < 1 day), creating a settlement fee jump at the breakpoint boundary that causes settlement fee overclaim or underclaim depending on whether the 0d fee is higher or lower than the 1d fee? Proof idea: test TTM = 86400 vs 86399, assert settlementFee() output differs, assert consumableUnits units taken in take() produce correct claimableSettlementFee.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken, result in the settlement fee being paid by the payer (buyerCallback if non-zero, else buyer for buy offers), and verify that the settlement fee is correctly attributed to claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] and not to the receiver (sellerAssets go to receiver, fee goes to contract)? Proof idea: trace all token transfers in take() for buy and sell offers with and without callbacks, assert payer pays buyerAssets total, receiver gets sellerAssets, contract gets buyerAssets - sellerAssets == claimableSettlementFee delta.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets sell offer (offer.buy=true) cause consumableUnits to return units based on sellerAssetsToUnits with sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee, while take() computes buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) = units.mulDivDown(offerPrice, WAD) for buy offers, causing the buyer (maker) to pay offerPrice per unit while the seller (taker) receives sellerPrice per unit, and verify that the settlement fee (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) is correctly charged to the buyer side and not the seller? Proof idea: fuzz buy offers, assert buyer pays buyerAssets, seller receives sellerAssets, claimableSettlementFee delta == buyerAssets - sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take / continuousFeeCredit] Can an unprivileged lender cause continuousFeeCredit to exceed the actual continuous fees owed by lenders by having a position where credit is reduced to 0 via take() (sellerCreditDecrease == sellerPos.credit) without _updatePosition being called for the seller (hasCredit is true so _updatePosition IS called), causing the accrued fee to be added to continuousFeeCredit while the seller's pendingFee is reduced by sellerPendingFeeDecrease, and verify that continuousFeeCredit == sum of all accruedFee from _updatePosition calls? Proof idea: track all _updatePosition calls and their accruedFee, assert continuousFeeCredit == sum(accruedFee) minus bad debt scaling.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken post-maturity (block.timestamp > market.maturity), use the 0d settlement fee breakpoint (settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP), while the offer was signed with the expectation of a higher TTM fee, causing the settlement fee to be underclaimed relative to the maker's intent and the taker to receive a better effective price than the offer intended? Proof idea: sign offer pre-maturity with high TTM fee, take post-maturity, assert claimableSettlementFee delta uses 0d fee, assert sellerAssets > expected for maker.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken with units > 0 but buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0 (when settlement fee rounds to 0 for very small units * settlementFee < WAD), cause claimableSettlementFee to not increase despite a trade occurring, resulting in unclaimable settlement fee for small trades? Proof idea: fuzz small unit counts with low settlement fee, assert claimableSettlementFee delta == 0 iff units * settlementFee < WAD, assert this is bounded by 1 wei per take.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken in a sequence of N partial fills, accumulate claimableSettlementFee that differs from a single full fill by more than N wei due to rounding in buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = units.mulDivDown(sellerPrice, WAD), causing systematic settlement fee underclaim or overclaim? Proof idea: fuzz N partial fills summing to total units, assert sum(buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) == single_fill(buyerAssets - sellerAssets) within N wei.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken, result in the buyer's credit increasing by units (not units - fee), while the buyer pays buyerAssets = units * buyerPrice / WAD, causing the buyer to receive full credit units while paying the settlement fee on top, and verify that the settlement fee is not double-counted in the buyer's credit or pendingFee? Proof idea: assert buyer credit increase == units, assert buyer pendingFee increase == buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * TTM / WAD, assert claimableSettlementFee delta == buyerAssets - sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken in MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with a non-zero referralFeePct, cause the referral fee (targetSellerAssets * referralFeePct / (WAD - referralFeePct)) to be added to targetFilledSellerAssets, causing the bundler to fill more units than intended and accumulate more settlement fee than the maker's maxAssets cap allows? Proof idea: set referralFeePct > 0, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget, assert consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxAssets and claimableSettlementFee delta is correct.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken, result in the settlement fee being accumulated in claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] for a market where the feeClaimer has been changed (old claimer loses unclaimed fees per NatSpec), causing the old claimer's accumulated settlement fees to be claimable by the new claimer, effectively charging the settlement fee to the wrong recipient? Proof idea: accumulate settlement fees, change feeClaimer, assert new claimer can claim all accumulated fees and old claimer cannot, assert no double-claim.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take / buyerPendingFeeIncrease] Can an unprivileged taker cause buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) to overflow uint256 when continuousFee (uint32, max ~317 per second) * timeToMaturity (max 100 years = ~3.15e9 seconds) * buyerCreditIncrease (max uint128) exceeds uint256, causing take() to revert and making the settlement fee from this offer permanently unclaimable? Proof idea: set continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, maturity = block.timestamp + 100 years, buyerCreditIncrease = type(uint128).max, assert no overflow in continuousFee * timeToMaturity * buyerCreditIncrease.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken in a sequence where the same maker's group is used across multiple markets with different settlement fees, cause consumed[maker][group] to be incremented by assets from different markets, leading to incorrect remaining capacity computation in consumableUnits and settlement fee mis-accounting? Proof idea: fill offer in market A (group G), call consumableUnits for offer in market B (same group G), assert consumed[maker][G] is shared and causes incorrect remaining capacity.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets buy offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken, result in the settlement fee being computed as buyerAssets - sellerAssets where both are rounded in the same direction (down for buy offers), causing the settlement fee to be systematically underclaimed by up to 1 wei per take, and over N takes this accumulates to a material underclaim? Proof idea: fuzz N takes with buy offers, assert sum(buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) >= N * (units_i * settlementFee / WAD - 1), assert underclaim bounded by N wei.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / take] Can an unprivileged taker under a maxAssets sell offer cause consumableUnits to return units that, when taken, result in the settlement fee being computed as buyerAssets - sellerAssets where both are rounded up (mulDivUp for sell offers), causing the settlement fee to be overclaimed by up to 1 wei per take when buyerAssets rounds up more than sellerAssets? Proof idea: fuzz N takes with sell offers, assert sum(buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) <= N * (units_i * settlementFee / WAD + 1), assert overclaim bounded by N wei.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: consumableUnits / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender cause the continuous fee accrual formula (postSlashPendingFee * (accrualEnd - lastAccrual) / (maturity - lastAccrual)) to produce 0 when accrualEnd - lastAccrual is very small relative to maturity - lastAccrual (e.g., 1 second accrual on a 100-year market), causing continuous fees to be unclaimable for short time intervals and accumulating to a material underclaim over many small interactions? Proof idea: fuzz small dt values relative to maturity - lastAccrual, assert fee > 0 iff postSlashPendingFee * dt >= maturity - lastAccrual, assert total underclaim bounded by N wei for N interactions.
